subject: Anthropology
title: Homo Erectus
description: A fast breakdown of Homo Erectus for builders.

hook.text: Homo erectus lasted nearly two million years. No other human species has come close to that endurance.

buildup.text: Erectus appeared about 1.9 million years ago with a larger brain, longer legs, and a body built for walking.

discovery.text: They were the first hominins to leave Africa, spreading to Asia and Europe with Acheulean hand axes.

twist.text: Erectus likely controlled fire, cooked food, and may have built shelters — a revolution in survival strategy.

climax.text: Homo erectus was the first global hominin. Their success proves that adaptability beats specialization.

punchline.text: They walked out of Africa and conquered the world.

quiz.question: What was a major achievement of Homo erectus?
options: They were the first to leave Africa
